text: In Agile Methodology, teams work in short cycles called sprints, usually lasting one to four weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning meeting where tasks are chosen from a list known as the backlog. Team members turn requirements into small pieces of work called user stories, which describe features from a user’s perspective. Every day, they hold a quick standup meeting to discuss progress and identify any obstacles. This approach is called iterative development because the product improves gradually through repeated cycles. After each sprint, the team reviews the work and adapts their plan. Agile helps teams respond to changes quickly and deliver value to customers more frequently.
